evals.prompt: I have a CSV file at /data/exports/tickets.csv with 25,000 rows and 45 columns. Can you analyze it for trends in ticket resolution times and category distributions?
evals.expected_output: Team Lead detects structured_data via .csv extension, partitions into ~17 chunks of ~1500 rows each (wide data: 45 columns), creates one task per chunk with identical query, spawns swarm:rlm-data-analyzer analysts, synthesizes aggregated findings.
expectations: Detects content type as structured_data with High confidence from .csv extension
Uses ~1500 row chunk size because the file has 45 columns (wide data, 20+ columns)
Creates approximately 17 partition tasks (25000 / 1500), NOT 1 task for the whole file
Every task contains the SAME query (trends in resolution times and category distributions) - tasks differ only by row range
Routes all analysts to swarm:rlm-data-analyzer, NOT swarm:rlm-chunk-analyzer
Preserves the CSV header row in every chunk file

evals.prompt: Analyze the Python file at /src/app/main.py - it's about 4200 lines. Look for security vulnerabilities.
evals.expected_output: Team Lead detects source_code from .py extension, partitions at function/class boundaries into 150-300 line chunks, sets analysis focus to 'security', spawns swarm:rlm-code-analyzer analysts, prepends import block to each chunk.
expectations: Detects content type as source_code with High confidence from .py extension
Partitions at function/class boundaries (def, class keywords at indentation level 0), NOT arbitrary line ranges
Chunk sizes stay within 150-300 lines; chunks exceeding 300 lines are split at inner boundaries
Extracts the import block from the file and prepends it to every chunk for dependency awareness
Sets analysis focus to 'security' in analyst prompts based on the user's goal
Routes analysts to swarm:rlm-code-analyzer, NOT swarm:rlm-chunk-analyzer

evals.prompt: I need you to analyze the error patterns in /var/log/app/server.log. The file is about 40,000 lines.
evals.expected_output: Team Lead detects log content type, partitions into line ranges with overlap, spawns swarm:rlm-chunk-analyzer analysts with chunk M of N indexing, synthesizes temporal patterns.
expectations: Detects content type as log with High confidence from .log extension
Partitions into line ranges targeting 5-10 partitions (200-5000 lines per chunk, adjustable)
Includes 20-50 lines of overlap between consecutive chunks to prevent splitting multi-line stack traces
Each analyst receives 'chunk M of N' indexing for temporal ordering
Routes analysts to swarm:rlm-chunk-analyzer
Uses Read offset/limit for partition method (no chunk files written to disk)

evals.prompt: Review the project directory at /project/src/ which has 3 Python files (2800, 1900, 3200 lines each), 2 JSON configs (250 and 180 lines), and a README.md (300 lines).
evals.expected_output: Multi-file RLM: enumerates files, partitions large Python files into ~200-line chunks at function boundaries, batches small JSON and prose files, spawns mixed analyst types, runs two-phase synthesis.
expectations: Uses multi-file RLM mode, not single-file mode, because directory contains mixed content types
Partitions each Python file into chunks using function/class boundaries (~150-300 line chunks), NOT one task per Python file
Batches the two small JSON files (250+180=430 lines, under 1500) into a single analyst task
The README (300 lines, under 1500) gets one analyst task as a lone small file
Spawns mixed analyst types simultaneously: swarm:rlm-code-analyzer for Python chunks, swarm:rlm-json-analyzer for JSON batch
Runs two-phase synthesis: Phase 1 parallel per-type synthesis, then Phase 2 cross-type synthesis

evals.prompt: Can you review this small utility file at /src/helpers.py? It's about 200 lines.
evals.expected_output: Team Lead determines the file is under the 1500-line threshold and does NOT use RLM. Reads and analyzes the file directly in context.
expectations: Does NOT invoke the RLM pattern because the file is ~200 lines, well under the ~1500 line threshold
Reads and analyzes the file directly without creating teams, partitions, or analyst agents
Does NOT create a TeamCreate call or spawn analyst teammates
Provides analysis results directly from the team lead's own reading of the file

evals.prompt: Analyze the error patterns in this log file. I want Task 1 to look at error frequencies, Task 2 to analyze root causes, Task 3 to find temporal trends, and Task 4 to synthesize. The file is /var/log/production.log with 20,000 lines.
evals.expected_output: Team Lead rejects the user's thematic decomposition and correctly applies data partitioning instead. Each task gets a chunk of lines with the SAME full query.
expectations: Does NOT create tasks named 'error frequencies', 'root causes', 'temporal trends' as separate analysis concerns
Recognizes the user's suggestion as thematic decomposition (the WRONG pattern) and applies data partitioning instead
Creates tasks based on line ranges (e.g., 'chunk 1 of N - lines 1-5000') where each task analyzes ALL aspects (errors, root causes, trends)
Every analyst task contains the SAME query covering all analysis goals - only the data range differs
Explains to the user why data partitioning is used instead of their suggested thematic approach

evals.prompt: Process the directory /data/exports/ which has 11 CSV files, each around 10,000 rows with about 2000 columns of Jira export data. Find patterns across all the data.
evals.expected_output: Multi-file RLM with mandatory CSV partitioning. Each CSV file is partitioned into ~1500-row chunks (wide/sparse data), producing ~77 total tasks across all files, with staged spawning of swarm:rlm-data-analyzer analysts.
expectations: Partitions EACH of the 11 CSV files into chunks - does NOT create just 11 tasks (one per file)
Uses ~1500 rows per chunk because the data is wide (2000+ columns) and sparse (Jira exports)
Total task count is approximately 73-77 (11 files x ~7 chunks each), significantly more than 11
Uses staged spawning in batches of ~15 analysts because total analyst count exceeds 50
All analysts are swarm:rlm-data-analyzer (single content type, no mixed analyst routing needed)
Only Phase 1 synthesis is needed (no Phase 2 cross-type synthesis since all files are the same type)

evals.prompt: I have a compressed file at /data/archive.tar.gz. Can you analyze its contents?
evals.expected_output: Team Lead extracts the archive first, then enumerates extracted files, detects content types, and applies appropriate single-file or multi-file RLM depending on what was inside.
expectations: Extracts the archive using 'tar xzf /data/archive.tar.gz -C /tmp/rlm-extract/' before any content-type detection
After extraction, enumerates the extracted files to determine if single-file or multi-file RLM is needed
If archive contains a single file, proceeds with single-file RLM on the extracted file
If archive contains multiple files, proceeds with multi-file directory analysis on the extraction directory
Content-type detection runs on extracted files, NOT on the .tar.gz itself
